rulings.Case Title: IN RE: U.S. SETTLEMENT FUNDING, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On First Amended Petition For Approval Of Transfer Of Structured Settlement Payment Rights
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 9, 2014, Line 3, PETITIONER U.S. SETTLEMENT FUNDING, LLC Ntc Of Hearing On First Amended Petition For Approval Of Transfer Of Structured Settlement Payment Rights Denied, without prejudice. In the proposed order submitted May 15, 2014, Petitioner emphasizes that the subject annuity payments are life contingent. (Proposed Order, p. 4:30 - 5:3.) However, in the First Amended Petition, Petitioner did not proffer as evidence the annuity policy or any other evidence in support of the definition of "life contingent" as used in the policy. rulings.Case Title: CAHILL CONTRACTORS, INC. VS. A&B PAINTING, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ; Table Of Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 9, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT FRAMCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION The motion is denied. Defendant Framco fails to maintain its summary adjudication burden. Church Street Housing Associates never agreed to waive that statutory statute of limitations as to Framco. There is no authority holding that a subcontractor can bind an owner by entering into an agreement with the general contractor. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CAHILL CONTRACTORS, INC. VS. A&B PAINTING, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION As To The Complaint Of Church Street Housing Associates
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 9, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT FRAMCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION As To The Complaint Of Church Street Housing Associates The motion is denied. Defendant Framco fails to maintain its summary adjudication burden. The court sustains Church Street Housing Association's objection to the Morrison declaration. Mr. Morrison does not properly authenticate the cited exhibits. In addition, there exists a triable issue of material fact when Church Street housing Association discovered or reasonably should have discovered the defects in this case. See Haddix Dec. rulings.Case Title: ROSARIO M. O'CONNOR et al VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn Compel Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. And Wells Fargo & Co.; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, June 9, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS ROSARIO O'CONNOR, C. PUNSALAN IRREVOCABLE TRUST DTD 11/12/2005, MARTIN CALIMPUSAN, SUSAN CALIMPUSAN, ANDREW CANANEA's Motion To Compel Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. And Wells Fargo & Co.; Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: OMNIMAGNET, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. JEFFREY L. GOODMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ; Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 9, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANT JEFFREY GOODMAN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Denied. Plaintiff has established that facts essential to justify opposition to the motion exist, but Plaintiff has been unable to recover those facts in discovery. (CCP sec. 437c(h).) Plaintiff's counsel states under penalty of perjury that completion of Defendant's deposition and responses to special interrogatories "are essential to opposing the motion since they go to the heart of breach of contract claims as well as misrepresentation and breach of fiduciary duty and duties of loyalty claims." (Declaration of Faryan Andrew Afifi, par. 9.) Additionally, Mr. Afifi declares that he has made reasonable efforts to obtain the relevant discovery, but has been unable to do so because of Defendant's unreasonable delays. (Id.) Defendant does not respond to Plaintiff's arguments about discovery in reply, except to state that discovery is now closed. This argument is unavailing, as denial of this motion will provide Plaintiff the opportunity to elicit testimony at trial. Additionally, the motion is denied on procedural grounds. Defendant did not comply with California Rule of Court 3.1350(i), which provides that "[o]n request, a party MUST within three days provide to the other party¿an electronic version of its separate statement." (Emphasis added.) The Court has discretion to deny a summary adjudication motion for failing to comply with the California Rules of Court. (Truong v. Glasser (2009) 181 Cal.App.4th 102, 118.) Given the close proximity of this motion to trial and the fact that Plaintiff's opposition was due near the close of discovery when the parties had to prepare for multiple depositions, Defendant placed an unnecessary burden on Plaintiff by not following the Rules of Court and requiring Defendant to re-create Plaintiff's separate statement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: SAVOY DEVELOPMENT LLC, VS. VALLEJO INVESTMENTS LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 9, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT VALLEJO INVESTMENTS LLC'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND Denied. Defendant Vallejo Investments, LLC has not demonstrated that it is entitled to relief pursuant to CCP § 473(b). The only evidence proffered by Defendant in support of the motion is the declaration of its counsel Gregory J. Brod. Mr. Brod has not established personal knowledge of any of the facts therein. Furthermore, Mr. Brod has not provided any facts upon which Defendant is entitled to relief pursuant to CCP § 473(b). The Court does not consider the evidence improperly filed in reply. rulings.Case Title: FRANKLIN H WRIGHT VS. MARRIOTT INTERNATIONAL, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 9, 2014, Line 14, DEFENDANT HOST HOTELS & RESORTS, INC'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT Granted. (CCP § 436.) The demurrer is untimely. "A party who has filed a complaint or cross-complaint may, within 10 days after service of the answer to his pleading, demur to the answer." (CCP § 430.40(b).) On March 18, 2014, Defendant Host Hotels & Resorts, filed and mail-served its Answer. On April 15, 2014, Plaintiff filed his demurrer to the Answer, which is untimely. Plaintiff served the demurrer by email, which is improper absent the parties' agreement. (CCP § 1010.6.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. FAGEL'S MOTION To Change Venue And Transfer Action To The Los Angeles Superior Court-Central District; Req. For Reasonable Expenses And Attorneys' Fees In The Amount Of $8,785.00 Against Pltf'S Counsel Continued on the court's own motion to June 17, 2014.
